title: Leakage-safe](https://www.kaggle.com/code/pilkwang/s6e5-driver-s-high-driver-feature-eng%22]},{%22title%22:%22Leakage-safe) categorical frequency encodings
summary: Add count and frequency encodings for high-cardinality categorical contexts without using the target.
rationale: Frequency encodings appeared in stronger synthesis-style feature stacks, and the raw data has many Driver values with no missing categorical values. Non-target frequencies can help tree models understand category reliability and train/test coverage.
implementation_hint: Compute train-only or combined train-test frequencies for Driver, Race, Race-Year, Compound, Driver-Year, and Race-Compound. Add log-count and rare-category flags while keeping raw categorical columns for CatBoost.
expected_effect: Small AUC/stability gain with medium confidence.
risk: Combined train-test frequencies are transductive; they avoid target leakage but exploit test-distribution availability. Compare train-only versus combined frequencies under the same validation scheme.
